{{Short description|Cluster of kofun burial mounds in Ōno, Chūbu, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = No Kofun Group | native_name = 野古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = No kofun Group CCB1987-1C2A9.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = No Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = No Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|48|N|136|37|31|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōno, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th to early 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''No Kofun Group'''|野古墳群| No Kofun-gun}} is cluster of small ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s located in the Ono neighborhood of the town of [[Ōno, Gifu|Ōno]], [[Ibi District, Gifu|Ibi District]], [[Gifu Prefecture]] in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190832|title= 下船塚古墳 |trans-title= No Kofun Group |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==Overview== The ''kofun'' group is located in the northwestern corner of the [[Nōbi Plain]] in northwestern Gifu Prefecture, surrounded by [[paddy field|rice fields]] and orchards. In a roughly one square kilometer area, there were once over 200 tumuli, of which 14 theoretically received protection in 1957 as a National Historic Site. These included 27 [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumuli]] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, or the similarly-shaped "{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}" ''kofun'' {{nihongo|''hotategai-gata-kofun''|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}). At present, only nine remain; with the others being destroyed for expansion of farm land. Of these remaining nine, eight have been excavated, and from the [[grave goods]] recovered (''[[haniwa]]'', [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, horse fittings, iron and wooden implements, iron swords and spearheads), were determined to date to the middle of the 5th century to the early part of the 6th century AD. One of the bronze mirrors is kept at the [[Gotoh Museum]] in [[Tokyo]]. The site is about eight minutes by car from [[Motosu Station]] on the [[Tarumi Railway Tarumi Line]].{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class=wikitable ! #||name ||photo ||type & Dimensions || Comments |- ||1||Motare Kofun
(モタレ古墳) ||[[file:No Kofungun 1go-fun.jpg|100px]]|| keyhole-shaped
54m (L) x 36m (D) x 6m (H) ||Originally thought to be circular, excavations in 1989 confirmed that it was a moated keyhole-shaped tomb. Fragments of ''haniwa'', [[Sue ware]] and a wooden plough were recovered. |- ||2||Fudozuka Kofun
(不動塚古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 2go-fun.jpg|100px]] || keyhole-shaped
64m (L) x 44m (D) x 7m (H) ||Excavations in 1989 found fragments of ''haniwa''; most of the mound has been lost |- |3||Minamiyashiki-nishi Kofun
(南屋敷西古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 3go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||keyhole-shaped
76m (L) x 44m (D) x 5.4m (H) ||Excavations in 1982 found that it was double-moated; fragments of cylindrical haniwa were found |- |4||Noborikoshi Kofun
(墳登越古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 4go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||keyhole-shaped

79m (L) x 49m (D) x 6.3m (H)||Thought to be the oldest in the group. Half-destroyed by residential housing; some haniwa have been recovered. |- |7|| -unnamed-||[[file:No Kofungun 7go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||scallop-shaped
29m (L) x 22.6m (D) x 4.2m (H) ||Thought to be a circular tumuli, its design was confirmed in 1923. |- |8|| -unnamed-||[[file:No Kofungun 8go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||square-shaped

{{Short description|Cluster of kofun burial mounds in Ōno, Chūbu, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = No Kofun Group | native_name = 野古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = No kofun Group CCB1987-1C2A9.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = No Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = No Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|48|N|136|37|31|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōno, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th to early 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''No Kofun Group'''|野古墳群| No Kofun-gun}} is cluster of small ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s located in the Ono neighborhood of the town of [[Ōno, Gifu|Ōno]], [[Ibi District, Gifu|Ibi District]], [[Gifu Prefecture]] in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190832|title= 下船塚古墳 |trans-title= No Kofun Group |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==Overview== The ''kofun'' group is located in the northwestern corner of the [[Nōbi Plain]] in northwestern Gifu Prefecture, surrounded by [[paddy field|rice fields]] and orchards. In a roughly one square kilometer area, there were once over 200 tumuli, of which 14 theoretically received protection in 1957 as a National Historic Site. These included 27 [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumuli]] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, or the similarly-shaped "{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}" ''kofun'' {{nihongo|''hotategai-gata-kofun''|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}). At present, only nine remain; with the others being destroyed for expansion of farm land. Of these remaining nine, eight have been excavated, and from the [[grave goods]] recovered (''[[haniwa]]'', [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, horse fittings, iron and wooden implements, iron swords and spearheads), were determined to date to the middle of the 5th century to the early part of the 6th century AD. One of the bronze mirrors is kept at the [[Gotoh Museum]] in [[Tokyo]]. The site is about eight minutes by car from [[Motosu Station]] on the [[Tarumi Railway Tarumi Line]].{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class=wikitable ! #||name ||photo ||type & Dimensions || Comments |- ||1||Motare Kofun
(モタレ古墳) ||[[file:No Kofungun 1go-fun.jpg|100px]]|| keyhole-shaped
54m (L) x 36m (D) x 6m (H) ||Originally thought to be circular, excavations in 1989 confirmed that it was a moated keyhole-shaped tomb. Fragments of ''haniwa'', [[Sue ware]] and a wooden plough were recovered. |- ||2||Fudozuka Kofun
(不動塚古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 2go-fun.jpg|100px]] || keyhole-shaped
64m (L) x 44m (D) x 7m (H) ||Excavations in 1989 found fragments of ''haniwa''; most of the mound has been lost |- |3||Minamiyashiki-nishi Kofun
(南屋敷西古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 3go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||keyhole-shaped
76m (L) x 44m (D) x 5.4m (H) ||Excavations in 1982 found that it was double-moated; fragments of cylindrical haniwa were found |- |4||Noborikoshi Kofun
(墳登越古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 4go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||keyhole-shaped
83m (L) x 52m (D) x 7.3m (H) ||The largest in the group. It was double-moated; fragments of cylindrical ''haniwa'' were found |- |5||Jōtsuka Kofun
(城塚古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 5go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||keyhole-shaped
75m (L) x 39m (D) x 6.2m (H)||In excavations, a Chinese-made bronze mirror, horse harness and a large sword were found in the burial chamber. |- |6||Inui Yashiki Kofun
(乾屋敷古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 6go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||keyhole-shaped
79m (L) x 49m (D) x 6.3m (H)||Thought to be the oldest in the group. Half-destroyed by residential housing; some haniwa have been recovered. |- |7|| -unnamed-||[[file:No Kofungun 7go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||scallop-shaped
29m (L) x 22.6m (D) x 4.2m (H) ||Thought to be a circular tumuli, its design was confirmed in 1923. |- |8|| -unnamed-||[[file:No Kofungun 8go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||square-shaped
15.8 (L) x 15.8m (D) x 3.0m (H) ||Excavated in 1994 |- |9|| -unnamed-||[[file:No Kofungun 9go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||keyhole-shaped
30.5 (L) x 23m (D) x 6m (H) ||Excavated in 1994, ''haniwa'', Sue pottery were uncovered. |- |- |} ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Short description|Cluster of kofun burial mounds in Ōno, Chūbu, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = No Kofun Group | native_name = 野古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = No kofun Group CCB1987-1C2A9.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = No Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = No Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|48|N|136|37|31|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōno, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th to early 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''No Kofun Group'''|野古墳群| No Kofun-gun}} is cluster of small ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s located in the Ono neighborhood of the town of [[Ōno, Gifu|Ōno]], [[Ibi District, Gifu|Ibi District]], [[Gifu Prefecture]] in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190832|title= 下船塚古墳 |trans-title= No Kofun Group |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==Overview== The ''kofun'' group is located in the northwestern corner of the [[Nōbi Plain]] in northwestern Gifu Prefecture, surrounded by [[paddy field|rice fields]] and orchards. In a roughly one square kilometer area, there were once over 200 tumuli, of which 14 theoretically received protection in 1957 as a National Historic Site. These included 27 [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumuli]] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, or the similarly-shaped "{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}" ''kofun'' {{nihongo|''hotategai-gata-kofun''|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}). At present, only nine remain; with the others being destroyed for expansion of farm land. Of these remaining nine, eight have been excavated, and from the [[grave goods]] recovered (''[[haniwa]]'', [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, horse fittings, iron and wooden implements, iron swords and spearheads), were determined to date to the middle of the 5th century to the early part of the 6th century AD. One of the bronze mirrors is kept at the [[Gotoh Museum]] in [[Tokyo]]. The site is about eight minutes by car from [[Motosu Station]] on the [[Tarumi Railway Tarumi Line]].{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class=wikitable ! #||name ||photo ||type & Dimensions || Comments |- ||1||Motare Kofun
(モタレ古墳) ||[[file:No Kofungun 1go-fun.jpg|100px]]|| keyhole-shaped
54m (L) x 36m (D) x 6m (H) ||Originally thought to be circular, excavations in 1989 confirmed that it was a moated keyhole-shaped tomb. Fragments of ''haniwa'', [[Sue ware]] and a wooden plough were recovered. |- ||2||Fudozuka Kofun
(不動塚古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 2go-fun.jpg|100px]] || keyhole-shaped
64m (L) x 44m (D) x 7m (H) ||Excavations in 1989 found fragments of ''haniwa''; most of the mound has been lost |- |3||Minamiyashiki-nishi Kofun
(南屋敷西古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 3go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||keyhole-shaped
76m (L) x 44m (D) x 5.4m (H) ||Excavations in 1982 found that it was double-moated; fragments of cylindrical haniwa were found |- |4||Noborikoshi Kofun
(墳登越古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 4go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||keyhole-shaped
83m (L) x 52m (D) x 7.3m (H) ||The largest in the group. It was double-moated; fragments of cylindrical ''haniwa'' were found |- |5||Jōtsuka Kofun
(城塚古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 5go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||keyhole-shaped
75m (L) x 39m (D) x 6.2m (H)||In excavations, a Chinese-made bronze mirror, horse harness and a large sword were found in the burial chamber. |- |6||Inui Yashiki Kofun
(乾屋敷古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 6go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||keyhole-shaped
79m (L) x 49m (D) x 6.3m (H)||Thought to be the oldest in the group. Half-destroyed by residential housing; some haniwa have been recovered. |- |7|| -unnamed-||[[file:No Kofungun 7go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||scallop-shaped
29m (L) x 22.6m (D) x 4.2m (H) ||Thought to be a circular tumuli, its design was confirmed in 1923. |- |8|| -unnamed-||[[file:No Kofungun 8go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||square-shaped
15.8 (L) x 15.8m (D) x 3.0m (H) ||Excavated in 1994 |- |9|| -unnamed-||[[file:No Kofungun 9go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||keyhole-shaped
30.5 (L) x 23m (D) x 6m (H) ||Excavated in 1994, ''haniwa'', Sue pottery were uncovered. |- |- |} ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Short description|Cluster of kofun burial mounds in Ōno, Chūbu, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = No Kofun Group | native_name = 野古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = No kofun Group CCB1987-1C2A9.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = No Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = No Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|48|N|136|37|31|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōno, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th to early 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''No Kofun Group'''|野古墳群| No Kofun-gun}} is cluster of small ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s located in the Ono neighborhood of the town of [[Ōno, Gifu|Ōno]], [[Ibi District, Gifu|Ibi District]], [[Gifu Prefecture]] in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190832|title= 下船塚古墳 |trans-title= No Kofun Group |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==Overview== The ''kofun'' group is located in the northwestern corner of the [[Nōbi Plain]] in northwestern Gifu Prefecture, surrounded by [[paddy field|rice fields]] and orchards. In a roughly one square kilometer area, there were once over 200 tumuli, of which 14 theoretically received protection in 1957 as a National Historic Site. These included 27 [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumuli]] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, or the similarly-shaped "{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}" ''kofun'' {{nihongo|''hotategai-gata-kofun''|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}). At present, only nine remain; with the others being destroyed for expansion of farm land. Of these remaining nine, eight have been excavated, and from the [[grave goods]] recovered (''[[haniwa]]'', [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, horse fittings, iron and wooden implements, iron swords and spearheads), were determined to date to the middle of the 5th century to the early part of the 6th century AD. One of the bronze mirrors is kept at the [[Gotoh Museum]] in [[Tokyo]]. The site is about eight minutes by car from [[Motosu Station]] on the [[Tarumi Railway Tarumi Line]].{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class=wikitable ! #||name ||photo ||type & Dimensions || Comments |- ||1||Motare Kofun

{{Short description|Cluster of kofun burial mounds in Ōno, Chūbu, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = No Kofun Group | native_name = 野古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = No kofun Group CCB1987-1C2A9.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = No Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = No Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|48|N|136|37|31|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōno, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th to early 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''No Kofun Group'''|野古墳群| No Kofun-gun}} is cluster of small ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s located in the Ono neighborhood of the town of [[Ōno, Gifu|Ōno]], [[Ibi District, Gifu|Ibi District]], [[Gifu Prefecture]] in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190832|title= 下船塚古墳 |trans-title= No Kofun Group |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==Overview== The ''kofun'' group is located in the northwestern corner of the [[Nōbi Plain]] in northwestern Gifu Prefecture, surrounded by [[paddy field|rice fields]] and orchards. In a roughly one square kilometer area, there were once over 200 tumuli, of which 14 theoretically received protection in 1957 as a National Historic Site. These included 27 [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumuli]] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, or the similarly-shaped "{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}" ''kofun'' {{nihongo|''hotategai-gata-kofun''|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}). At present, only nine remain; with the others being destroyed for expansion of farm land. Of these remaining nine, eight have been excavated, and from the [[grave goods]] recovered (''[[haniwa]]'', [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, horse fittings, iron and wooden implements, iron swords and spearheads), were determined to date to the middle of the 5th century to the early part of the 6th century AD. One of the bronze mirrors is kept at the [[Gotoh Museum]] in [[Tokyo]]. The site is about eight minutes by car from [[Motosu Station]] on the [[Tarumi Railway Tarumi Line]].{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class=wikitable ! #||name ||photo ||type & Dimensions || Comments |- ||1||Motare Kofun
(モタレ古墳) ||[[file:No Kofungun 1go-fun.jpg|100px]]|| keyhole-shaped
54m (L) x 36m (D) x 6m (H) ||Originally thought to be circular, excavations in 1989 confirmed that it was a moated keyhole-shaped tomb. Fragments of ''haniwa'', [[Sue ware]] and a wooden plough were recovered. |- ||2||Fudozuka Kofun
(不動塚古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 2go-fun.jpg|100px]] || keyhole-shaped
64m (L) x 44m (D) x 7m (H) ||Excavations in 1989 found fragments of ''haniwa''; most of the mound has been lost |- |3||Minamiyashiki-nishi Kofun
(南屋敷西古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 3go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||keyhole-shaped
76m (L) x 44m (D) x 5.4m (H) ||Excavations in 1982 found that it was double-moated; fragments of cylindrical haniwa were found |- |4||Noborikoshi Kofun
(墳登越古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 4go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||keyhole-shaped
83m (L) x 52m (D) x 7.3m (H) ||The largest in the group. It was double-moated; fragments of cylindrical ''haniwa'' were found |- |5||Jōtsuka Kofun
(城塚古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 5go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||keyhole-shaped
75m (L) x 39m (D) x 6.2m (H)||In excavations, a Chinese-made bronze mirror, horse harness and a large sword were found in the burial chamber. |- |6||Inui Yashiki Kofun
(乾屋敷古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 6go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||keyhole-shaped
79m (L) x 49m (D) x 6.3m (H)||Thought to be the oldest in the group. Half-destroyed by residential housing; some haniwa have been recovered. |- |7|| -unnamed-||[[file:No Kofungun 7go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||scallop-shaped
29m (L) x 22.6m (D) x 4.2m (H) ||Thought to be a circular tumuli, its design was confirmed in 1923. |- |8|| -unnamed-||[[file:No Kofungun 8go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||square-shaped
15.8 (L) x 15.8m (D) x 3.0m (H) ||Excavated in 1994 |- |9|| -unnamed-||[[file:No Kofungun 9go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||keyhole-shaped

{{Short description|Cluster of kofun burial mounds in Ōno, Chūbu, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = No Kofun Group | native_name = 野古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = No kofun Group CCB1987-1C2A9.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = No Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = No Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|48|N|136|37|31|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōno, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th to early 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''No Kofun Group'''|野古墳群| No Kofun-gun}} is cluster of small ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s located in the Ono neighborhood of the town of [[Ōno, Gifu|Ōno]], [[Ibi District, Gifu|Ibi District]], [[Gifu Prefecture]] in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190832|title= 下船塚古墳 |trans-title= No Kofun Group |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==Overview== The ''kofun'' group is located in the northwestern corner of the [[Nōbi Plain]] in northwestern Gifu Prefecture, surrounded by [[paddy field|rice fields]] and orchards. In a roughly one square kilometer area, there were once over 200 tumuli, of which 14 theoretically received protection in 1957 as a National Historic Site. These included 27 [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumuli]] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, or the similarly-shaped "{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}" ''kofun'' {{nihongo|''hotategai-gata-kofun''|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}). At present, only nine remain; with the others being destroyed for expansion of farm land. Of these remaining nine, eight have been excavated, and from the [[grave goods]] recovered (''[[haniwa]]'', [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, horse fittings, iron and wooden implements, iron swords and spearheads), were determined to date to the middle of the 5th century to the early part of the 6th century AD. One of the bronze mirrors is kept at the [[Gotoh Museum]] in [[Tokyo]]. The site is about eight minutes by car from [[Motosu Station]] on the [[Tarumi Railway Tarumi Line]].{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class=wikitable ! #||name ||photo ||type & Dimensions || Comments |- ||1||Motare Kofun
(モタレ古墳) ||[[file:No Kofungun 1go-fun.jpg|100px]]|| keyhole-shaped
54m (L) x 36m (D) x 6m (H) ||Originally thought to be circular, excavations in 1989 confirmed that it was a moated keyhole-shaped tomb. Fragments of ''haniwa'', [[Sue ware]] and a wooden plough were recovered. |- ||2||Fudozuka Kofun
(不動塚古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 2go-fun.jpg|100px]] || keyhole-shaped
64m (L) x 44m (D) x 7m (H) ||Excavations in 1989 found fragments of ''haniwa''; most of the mound has been lost |- |3||Minamiyashiki-nishi Kofun
(南屋敷西古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 3go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||keyhole-shaped
76m (L) x 44m (D) x 5.4m (H) ||Excavations in 1982 found that it was double-moated; fragments of cylindrical haniwa were found |- |4||Noborikoshi Kofun
(墳登越古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 4go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||keyhole-shaped
83m (L) x 52m (D) x 7.3m (H) ||The largest in the group. It was double-moated; fragments of cylindrical ''haniwa'' were found |- |5||Jōtsuka Kofun
(城塚古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 5go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||keyhole-shaped
75m (L) x 39m (D) x 6.2m (H)||In excavations, a Chinese-made bronze mirror, horse harness and a large sword were found in the burial chamber. |- |6||Inui Yashiki Kofun
(乾屋敷古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 6go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||keyhole-shaped
79m (L) x 49m (D) x 6.3m (H)||Thought to be the oldest in the group. Half-destroyed by residential housing; some haniwa have been recovered. |- |7|| -unnamed-||[[file:No Kofungun 7go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||scallop-shaped
29m (L) x 22.6m (D) x 4.2m (H) ||Thought to be a circular tumuli, its design was confirmed in 1923. |- |8|| -unnamed-||[[file:No Kofungun 8go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||square-shaped
15.8 (L) x 15.8m (D) x 3.0m (H) ||Excavated in 1994 |- |9|| -unnamed-||[[file:No Kofungun 9go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||keyhole-shaped
30.5 (L) x 23m (D) x 6m (H) ||Excavated in 1994, ''haniwa'', Sue pottery were uncovered. |- |- |} ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190832|title= 下船塚古墳 |trans-title= No Kofun Group |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==Overview== The ''kofun'' group is located in the northwestern corner of the [[Nōbi Plain]] in northwestern Gifu Prefecture, surrounded by [[paddy field|rice fields]] and orchards. In a roughly one square kilometer area, there were once over 200 tumuli, of which 14 theoretically received protection in 1957 as a National Historic Site. These included 27 [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumuli]] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, or the similarly-shaped "{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}" ''kofun'' {{nihongo|''hotategai-gata-kofun''|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}). At present, only nine remain; with the others being destroyed for expansion of farm land. One of the bronze mirrors is kept at the [[Gotoh Museum]] in [[Tokyo]]. {{Short description|Cluster of kofun burial mounds in Ōno, Chūbu, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = No Kofun Group | native_name = 野古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = No kofun Group CCB1987-1C2A9.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = No Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = No Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|48|N|136|37|31|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōno, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th to early 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''No Kofun Group'''|野古墳群| No Kofun-gun}} is cluster of small ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s located in the Ono neighborhood of the town of [[Ōno, Gifu|Ōno]], [[Ibi District, Gifu|Ibi District]], [[Gifu Prefecture]] in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190832|title= 下船塚古墳 |trans-title= No Kofun Group |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==Overview== The ''kofun'' group is located in the northwestern corner of the [[Nōbi Plain]] in northwestern Gifu Prefecture, surrounded by [[paddy field|rice fields]] and orchards. In a roughly one square kilometer area, there were once over 200 tumuli, of which 14 theoretically received protection in 1957 as a National Historic Site. These included 27 [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumuli]] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, or the similarly-shaped "{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}" ''kofun'' {{nihongo|''hotategai-gata-kofun''|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}). At present, only nine remain; with the others being destroyed for expansion of farm land. Of these remaining nine, eight have been excavated, and from the [[grave goods]] recovered (''[[haniwa]]'', [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, horse fittings, iron and wooden implements, iron swords and spearheads), were determined to date to the middle of the 5th century to the early part of the 6th century AD. One of the bronze mirrors is kept at the [[Gotoh Museum]] in [[Tokyo]]. The site is about eight minutes by car from [[Motosu Station]] on the [[Tarumi Railway Tarumi Line]].{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class=wikitable ! #||name ||photo ||type & Dimensions || Comments |- ||1||Motare Kofun
(モタレ古墳) ||[[file:No Kofungun 1go-fun.jpg|100px]]|| keyhole-shaped
54m (L) x 36m (D) x 6m (H) ||Originally thought to be circular, excavations in 1989 confirmed that it was a moated keyhole-shaped tomb. Fragments of ''haniwa'', [[Sue ware]] and a wooden plough were recovered. |- ||2||Fudozuka Kofun
(不動塚古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 2go-fun.jpg|100px]] || keyhole-shaped
64m (L) x 44m (D) x 7m (H) ||Excavations in 1989 found fragments of ''haniwa''; most of the mound has been lost |- |3||Minamiyashiki-nishi Kofun
(南屋敷西古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 3go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||keyhole-shaped
76m (L) x 44m (D) x 5.4m (H) ||Excavations in 1982 found that it was double-moated; fragments of cylindrical haniwa were found |- |4||Noborikoshi Kofun
(墳登越古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 4go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||keyhole-shaped

79m (L) x 49m (D) x 6.3m (H)||Thought to be the oldest in the group. Half-destroyed by residential housing; some haniwa have been recovered. |- |7|| -unnamed-||[[file:No Kofungun 7go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||scallop-shaped
29m (L) x 22.6m (D) x 4.2m (H) ||Thought to be a circular tumuli, its design was confirmed in 1923. |- |8|| -unnamed-||[[file:No Kofungun 8go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||square-shaped

{{Short description|Cluster of kofun burial mounds in Ōno, Chūbu, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = No Kofun Group | native_name = 野古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = No kofun Group CCB1987-1C2A9.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = No Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = No Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|48|N|136|37|31|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōno, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th to early 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''No Kofun Group'''|野古墳群| No Kofun-gun}} is cluster of small ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s located in the Ono neighborhood of the town of [[Ōno, Gifu|Ōno]], [[Ibi District, Gifu|Ibi District]], [[Gifu Prefecture]] in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190832|title= 下船塚古墳 |trans-title= No Kofun Group |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==Overview== The ''kofun'' group is located in the northwestern corner of the [[Nōbi Plain]] in northwestern Gifu Prefecture, surrounded by [[paddy field|rice fields]] and orchards. In a roughly one square kilometer area, there were once over 200 tumuli, of which 14 theoretically received protection in 1957 as a National Historic Site. These included 27 [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumuli]] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, or the similarly-shaped "{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}" ''kofun'' {{nihongo|''hotategai-gata-kofun''|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}). At present, only nine remain; with the others being destroyed for expansion of farm land. Of these remaining nine, eight have been excavated, and from the [[grave goods]] recovered (''[[haniwa]]'', [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, horse fittings, iron and wooden implements, iron swords and spearheads), were determined to date to the middle of the 5th century to the early part of the 6th century AD. One of the bronze mirrors is kept at the [[Gotoh Museum]] in [[Tokyo]]. The site is about eight minutes by car from [[Motosu Station]] on the [[Tarumi Railway Tarumi Line]].{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class=wikitable ! #||name ||photo ||type & Dimensions || Comments |- ||1||Motare Kofun
(モタレ古墳) ||[[file:No Kofungun 1go-fun.jpg|100px]]|| keyhole-shaped
54m (L) x 36m (D) x 6m (H) ||Originally thought to be circular, excavations in 1989 confirmed that it was a moated keyhole-shaped tomb. Fragments of ''haniwa'', [[Sue ware]] and a wooden plough were recovered. |- ||2||Fudozuka Kofun
(不動塚古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 2go-fun.jpg|100px]] || keyhole-shaped

(南屋敷西古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 3go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||keyhole-shaped
76m (L) x 44m (D) x 5.4m (H) ||Excavations in 1982 found that it was double-moated; fragments of cylindrical haniwa were found |- |4||Noborikoshi Kofun
(墳登越古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 4go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||keyhole-shaped
83m (L) x 52m (D) x 7.3m (H) ||The largest in the group. It was double-moated; fragments of cylindrical ''haniwa'' were found |- |5||Jōtsuka Kofun

79m (L) x 49m (D) x 6.3m (H)||Thought to be the oldest in the group. Half-destroyed by residential housing; some haniwa have been recovered. |- |7|| -unnamed-||[[file:No Kofungun 7go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||scallop-shaped
29m (L) x 22.6m (D) x 4.2m (H) ||Thought to be a circular tumuli, its design was confirmed in 1923. |- |8|| -unnamed-||[[file:No Kofungun 8go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||square-shaped

{{Short description|Cluster of kofun burial mounds in Ōno, Chūbu, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = No Kofun Group | native_name = 野古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = No kofun Group CCB1987-1C2A9.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = No Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = No Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|48|N|136|37|31|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōno, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th to early 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''No Kofun Group'''|野古墳群| No Kofun-gun}} is cluster of small ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s located in the Ono neighborhood of the town of [[Ōno, Gifu|Ōno]], [[Ibi District, Gifu|Ibi District]], [[Gifu Prefecture]] in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190832|title= 下船塚古墳 |trans-title= No Kofun Group |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==Overview== The ''kofun'' group is located in the northwestern corner of the [[Nōbi Plain]] in northwestern Gifu Prefecture, surrounded by [[paddy field|rice fields]] and orchards. In a roughly one square kilometer area, there were once over 200 tumuli, of which 14 theoretically received protection in 1957 as a National Historic Site. These included 27 [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumuli]] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, or the similarly-shaped "{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}" ''kofun'' {{nihongo|''hotategai-gata-kofun''|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}). At present, only nine remain; with the others being destroyed for expansion of farm land. Of these remaining nine, eight have been excavated, and from the [[grave goods]] recovered (''[[haniwa]]'', [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, horse fittings, iron and wooden implements, iron swords and spearheads), were determined to date to the middle of the 5th century to the early part of the 6th century AD. One of the bronze mirrors is kept at the [[Gotoh Museum]] in [[Tokyo]]. The site is about eight minutes by car from [[Motosu Station]] on the [[Tarumi Railway Tarumi Line]].{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class=wikitable ! #||name ||photo ||type & Dimensions || Comments |- ||1||Motare Kofun
(モタレ古墳) ||[[file:No Kofungun 1go-fun.jpg|100px]]|| keyhole-shaped
54m (L) x 36m (D) x 6m (H) ||Originally thought to be circular, excavations in 1989 confirmed that it was a moated keyhole-shaped tomb. Fragments of ''haniwa'', [[Sue ware]] and a wooden plough were recovered. |- ||2||Fudozuka Kofun
(不動塚古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 2go-fun.jpg|100px]] || keyhole-shaped
64m (L) x 44m (D) x 7m (H) ||Excavations in 1989 found fragments of ''haniwa''; most of the mound has been lost |- |3||Minamiyashiki-nishi Kofun
(南屋敷西古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 3go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||keyhole-shaped
76m (L) x 44m (D) x 5.4m (H) ||Excavations in 1982 found that it was double-moated; fragments of cylindrical haniwa were found |- |4||Noborikoshi Kofun
(墳登越古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 4go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||keyhole-shaped
83m (L) x 52m (D) x 7.3m (H) ||The largest in the group. It was double-moated; fragments of cylindrical ''haniwa'' were found |- |5||Jōtsuka Kofun

75m (L) x 39m (D) x 6.2m (H)||In excavations, a Chinese-made bronze mirror, horse harness and a large sword were found in the burial chamber. |- |6||Inui Yashiki Kofun
(乾屋敷古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 6go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||keyhole-shaped

{{Short description|Cluster of kofun burial mounds in Ōno, Chūbu, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = No Kofun Group | native_name = 野古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = No kofun Group CCB1987-1C2A9.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = No Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = No Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|48|N|136|37|31|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōno, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th to early 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''No Kofun Group'''|野古墳群| No Kofun-gun}} is cluster of small ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s located in the Ono neighborhood of the town of [[Ōno, Gifu|Ōno]], [[Ibi District, Gifu|Ibi District]], [[Gifu Prefecture]] in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190832|title= 下船塚古墳 |trans-title= No Kofun Group |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==Overview== The ''kofun'' group is located in the northwestern corner of the [[Nōbi Plain]] in northwestern Gifu Prefecture, surrounded by [[paddy field|rice fields]] and orchards. In a roughly one square kilometer area, there were once over 200 tumuli, of which 14 theoretically received protection in 1957 as a National Historic Site. These included 27 [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumuli]] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, or the similarly-shaped "{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}" ''kofun'' {{nihongo|''hotategai-gata-kofun''|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}). At present, only nine remain; with the others being destroyed for expansion of farm land. Of these remaining nine, eight have been excavated, and from the [[grave goods]] recovered (''[[haniwa]]'', [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, horse fittings, iron and wooden implements, iron swords and spearheads), were determined to date to the middle of the 5th century to the early part of the 6th century AD. One of the bronze mirrors is kept at the [[Gotoh Museum]] in [[Tokyo]]. The site is about eight minutes by car from [[Motosu Station]] on the [[Tarumi Railway Tarumi Line]].{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class=wikitable ! #||name ||photo ||type & Dimensions || Comments |- ||1||Motare Kofun

The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190832|title= 下船塚古墳 |trans-title= No Kofun Group |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==Overview== The ''kofun'' group is located in the northwestern corner of the [[Nōbi Plain]] in northwestern Gifu Prefecture, surrounded by [[paddy field|rice fields]] and orchards. In a roughly one square kilometer area, there were once over 200 tumuli, of which 14 theoretically received protection in 1957 as a National Historic Site. These included 27 [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumuli]] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, or the similarly-shaped "{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}" ''kofun'' {{nihongo|''hotategai-gata-kofun''|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}). One of the bronze mirrors is kept at the [[Gotoh Museum]] in [[Tokyo]]. {{Short description|Cluster of kofun burial mounds in Ōno, Chūbu, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = No Kofun Group | native_name = 野古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = No kofun Group CCB1987-1C2A9.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = No Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = No Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|48|N|136|37|31|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōno, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th to early 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''No Kofun Group'''|野古墳群| No Kofun-gun}} is cluster of small ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s located in the Ono neighborhood of the town of [[Ōno, Gifu|Ōno]], [[Ibi District, Gifu|Ibi District]], [[Gifu Prefecture]] in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190832|title= 下船塚古墳 |trans-title= No Kofun Group |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==Overview== The ''kofun'' group is located in the northwestern corner of the [[Nōbi Plain]] in northwestern Gifu Prefecture, surrounded by [[paddy field|rice fields]] and orchards. In a roughly one square kilometer area, there were once over 200 tumuli, of which 14 theoretically received protection in 1957 as a National Historic Site. These included 27 [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumuli]] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, or the similarly-shaped "{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}" ''kofun'' {{nihongo|''hotategai-gata-kofun''|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}). At present, only nine remain; with the others being destroyed for expansion of farm land. Of these remaining nine, eight have been excavated, and from the [[grave goods]] recovered (''[[haniwa]]'', [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, horse fittings, iron and wooden implements, iron swords and spearheads), were determined to date to the middle of the 5th century to the early part of the 6th century AD. One of the bronze mirrors is kept at the [[Gotoh Museum]] in [[Tokyo]]. The site is about eight minutes by car from [[Motosu Station]] on the [[Tarumi Railway Tarumi Line]].{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class=wikitable ! #||name ||photo ||type & Dimensions || Comments |- ||1||Motare Kofun
(モタレ古墳) ||[[file:No Kofungun 1go-fun.jpg|100px]]|| keyhole-shaped
54m (L) x 36m (D) x 6m (H) ||Originally thought to be circular, excavations in 1989 confirmed that it was a moated keyhole-shaped tomb. Fragments of ''haniwa'', [[Sue ware]] and a wooden plough were recovered. |- ||2||Fudozuka Kofun
(不動塚古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 2go-fun.jpg|100px]] || keyhole-shaped

(南屋敷西古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 3go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||keyhole-shaped
76m (L) x 44m (D) x 5.4m (H) ||Excavations in 1982 found that it was double-moated; fragments of cylindrical haniwa were found |- |4||Noborikoshi Kofun
(墳登越古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 4go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||keyhole-shaped
83m (L) x 52m (D) x 7.3m (H) ||The largest in the group. It was double-moated; fragments of cylindrical ''haniwa'' were found |- |5||Jōtsuka Kofun
(城塚古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 5go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||keyhole-shaped
75m (L) x 39m (D) x 6.2m (H)||In excavations, a Chinese-made bronze mirror, horse harness and a large sword were found in the burial chamber. |- |6||Inui Yashiki Kofun

79m (L) x 49m (D) x 6.3m (H)||Thought to be the oldest in the group. Half-destroyed by residential housing; some haniwa have been recovered. |- |7|| -unnamed-||[[file:No Kofungun 7go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||scallop-shaped
29m (L) x 22.6m (D) x 4.2m (H) ||Thought to be a circular tumuli, its design was confirmed in 1923. |- |8|| -unnamed-||[[file:No Kofungun 8go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||square-shaped
15.8 (L) x 15.8m (D) x 3.0m (H) ||Excavated in 1994 |- |9|| -unnamed-||[[file:No Kofungun 9go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||keyhole-shaped

{{Short description|Cluster of kofun Kofuns in Ōno, Chūbu, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = No Kofun Group | native_name = 野古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = No kofun Group CCB1987-1C2A9.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = No Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = No Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|48|N|136|37|31|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōno, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th to early 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''No Kofun Group'''|野古墳群| No Kofun-gun}} is cluster of small ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Ono neighborhood of the town of [[Ōno, Gifu|Ōno]], [[Ibi District, Gifu|Ibi District]], [[Gifu Prefecture]] in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190832|title= 下船塚古墳 |trans-title= No Kofun Group |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==Overview== The ''kofun'' group is located in the northwestern corner of the [[Nōbi Plain]] in northwestern Gifu Prefecture, surrounded by [[paddy field|rice fields]] and orchards. In a roughly one square kilometer area, there were once over 200 tumuli, of which 14 theoretically received protection in 1957 as a National Historic Site. These included 27 [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumuli]] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, or the similarly-shaped "{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}" ''kofun'' {{nihongo|''hotategai-gata-kofun''|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}). At present, only nine remain; with the others being destroyed for expansion of farm land. Of these remaining nine, eight have been excavated, and from the [[grave goods]] recovered (''[[haniwa]]'', [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, horse fittings, iron and wooden implements, iron swords and spearheads), were determined to date to the middle of the 5th century to the early part of the 6th century AD. One of the bronze mirrors is kept at the [[Gotoh Museum]] in [[Tokyo]]. The site is about eight minutes by car from [[Motosu Station]] on the [[Tarumi Railway Tarumi Line]].{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class=wikitable ! #||name ||photo ||type & Dimensions || Comments |- ||1||Motare Kofun
(モタレ古墳) ||[[file:No Kofungun 1go-fun.jpg|100px]]|| keyhole-shaped
54m (L) x 36m (D) x 6m (H) ||Originally thought to be circular, excavations in 1989 confirmed that it was a moated keyhole-shaped tomb. Fragments of ''haniwa'', [[Sue ware]] and a wooden plough were recovered. |- ||2||Fudozuka Kofun
(不動塚古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 2go-fun.jpg|100px]] || keyhole-shaped
64m (L) x 44m (D) x 7m (H) ||Excavations in 1989 found fragments of ''haniwa''; most of the mound has been lost |- |3||Minamiyashiki-nishi Kofun
(南屋敷西古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 3go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||keyhole-shaped
76m (L) x 44m (D) x 5.4m (H) ||Excavations in 1982 found that it was double-moated; fragments of cylindrical haniwa were found |- |4||Noborikoshi Kofun
(墳登越古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 4go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||keyhole-shaped
83m (L) x 52m (D) x 7.3m (H) ||The largest in the group. It was double-moated; fragments of cylindrical ''haniwa'' were found |- |5||Jōtsuka Kofun
(城塚古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 5go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||keyhole-shaped
75m (L) x 39m (D) x 6.2m (H)||In excavations, a Chinese-made bronze mirror, horse harness and a large sword were found in the burial chamber. |- |6||Inui Yashiki Kofun
(乾屋敷古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 6go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||keyhole-shaped
79m (L) x 49m (D) x 6.3m (H)||Thought to be the oldest in the group. Half-destroyed by residential housing; some haniwa have been recovered. |- |7|| -unnamed-||[[file:No Kofungun 7go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||scallop-shaped
29m (L) x 22.6m (D) x 4.2m (H) ||Thought to be a circular tumuli, its design was confirmed in 1923. |- |8|| -unnamed-||[[file:No Kofungun 8go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||square-shaped
15.8 (L) x 15.8m (D) x 3.0m (H) ||Excavated in 1994 |- |9|| -unnamed-||[[file:No Kofungun 9go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||keyhole-shaped
30.5 (L) x 23m (D) x 6m (H) ||Excavated in 1994, ''haniwa'', Sue pottery were uncovered. |- |- |} ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Short description|Cluster of kofun Kofuns in Ōno, Chūbu, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = No Kofun Group | native_name = 野古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = No kofun Group CCB1987-1C2A9.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = No Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = No Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|48|N|136|37|31|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōno, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th to early 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''No Kofun Group'''|野古墳群| No Kofun-gun}} is cluster of small ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Ono neighborhood of the town of [[Ōno, Gifu|Ōno]], [[Ibi District, Gifu|Ibi District]], [[Gifu Prefecture]] in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190832|title= 下船塚古墳 |trans-title= No Kofun Group |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==Overview== The ''kofun'' group is located in the northwestern corner of the [[Nōbi Plain]] in northwestern Gifu Prefecture, surrounded by [[paddy field|rice fields]] and orchards. In a roughly one square kilometer area, there were once over 200 tumuli, of which 14 theoretically received protection in 1957 as a National Historic Site. These included 27 [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumuli]] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, or the similarly-shaped "{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}" ''kofun'' {{nihongo|''hotategai-gata-kofun''|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}). At present, only nine remain; with the others being destroyed for expansion of farm land. Of these remaining nine, eight have been excavated, and from the [[grave goods]] recovered (''[[haniwa]]'', [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, horse fittings, iron and wooden implements, iron swords and spearheads), were determined to date to the middle of the 5th century to the early part of the 6th century AD. One of the bronze mirrors is kept at the [[Gotoh Museum]] in [[Tokyo]]. The site is about eight minutes by car from [[Motosu Station]] on the [[Tarumi Railway Tarumi Line]].{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class=wikitable ! #||name ||photo ||type & Dimensions || Comments |- ||1||Motare Kofun
(モタレ古墳) ||[[file:No Kofungun 1go-fun.jpg|100px]]|| keyhole-shaped
54m (L) x 36m (D) x 6m (H) ||Originally thought to be circular, excavations in 1989 confirmed that it was a moated keyhole-shaped tomb. Fragments of ''haniwa'', [[Sue ware]] and a wooden plough were recovered. |- ||2||Fudozuka Kofun

(南屋敷西古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 3go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||keyhole-shaped
76m (L) x 44m (D) x 5.4m (H) ||Excavations in 1982 found that it was double-moated; fragments of cylindrical haniwa were found |- |4||Noborikoshi Kofun
(墳登越古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 4go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||keyhole-shaped

{{Short description|Cluster of kofun Kofuns in Ōno, Chūbu, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | name = No Kofun Group | native_name = 野古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = No kofun Group CCB1987-1C2A9.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = No Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = No Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|48|N|136|37|31|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōno, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th to early 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''No Kofun Group'''|野古墳群| No Kofun-gun}} is cluster of small ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Ono neighborhood of the town of [[Ōno, Gifu|Ōno]], [[Ibi District, Gifu|Ibi District]], [[Gifu Prefecture]] in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190832|title= 下船塚古墳 |trans-title= No Kofun Group |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==Overview== The ''kofun'' group is located in the northwestern corner of the [[Nōbi Plain]] in northwestern Gifu Prefecture, surrounded by [[paddy field|rice fields]] and orchards. In a roughly one square kilometer area, there were once over 200 tumuli, of which 14 theoretically received protection in 1957 as a National Historic Site. These included 27 [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumuli]] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, or the similarly-shaped "{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}" ''kofun'' {{nihongo|''hotategai-gata-kofun''|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}). At present, only nine remain; with the others being destroyed for expansion of farm land. Of these remaining nine, eight have been excavated, and from the [[grave goods]] recovered (''[[haniwa]]'', [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, horse fittings, iron and wooden implements, iron swords and spearheads), were determined to date to the middle of the 5th century to the early part of the 6th century AD. One of the bronze mirrors is kept at the [[Gotoh Museum]] in [[Tokyo]]. The site is about eight minutes by car from [[Motosu Station]] on the [[Tarumi Railway Tarumi Line]].{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class=wikitable ! #||name ||photo ||type & Dimensions || Comments |- ||1||Motare Kofun
(モタレ古墳) ||[[file:No Kofungun 1go-fun.jpg|100px]]|| keyhole-shaped
54m (L) x 36m (D) x 6m (H) ||Originally thought to be circular, excavations in 1989 confirmed that it was a moated keyhole-shaped tomb. Fragments of ''haniwa'', [[Sue ware]] and a wooden plough were recovered. |- ||2||Fudozuka Kofun
(不動塚古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 2go-fun.jpg|100px]] || keyhole-shaped
64m (L) x 44m (D) x 7m (H) ||Excavations in 1989 found fragments of ''haniwa''; most of the mound has been lost |- |3||Minamiyashiki-nishi Kofun
(南屋敷西古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 3go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||keyhole-shaped
76m (L) x 44m (D) x 5.4m (H) ||Excavations in 1982 found that it was double-moated; fragments of cylindrical haniwa were found |- |4||Noborikoshi Kofun
(墳登越古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 4go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||keyhole-shaped
83m (L) x 52m (D) x 7.3m (H) ||The largest in the group. It was double-moated; fragments of cylindrical ''haniwa'' were found |- |5||Jōtsuka Kofun
(城塚古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 5go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||keyhole-shaped
75m (L) x 39m (D) x 6.2m (H)||In excavations, a Chinese-made bronze mirror, horse harness and a large sword were found in the burial chamber. |- |6||Inui Yashiki Kofun
(乾屋敷古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 6go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||keyhole-shaped
79m (L) x 49m (D) x 6.3m (H)||Thought to be the oldest in the group. Half-destroyed by residential housing; some haniwa have been recovered. |- |7|| -unnamed-||[[file:No Kofungun 7go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||scallop-shaped
29m (L) x 22.6m (D) x 4.2m (H) ||Thought to be a circular tumuli, its design was confirmed in 1923. |- |8|| -unnamed-||[[file:No Kofungun 8go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||square-shaped
15.8 (L) x 15.8m (D) x 3.0m (H) ||Excavated in 1994 |- |9|| -unnamed-||[[file:No Kofungun 9go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||keyhole-shaped
30.5 (L) x 23m (D) x 6m (H) ||Excavated in 1994, ''haniwa'', Sue pottery were uncovered. |- |- |} ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Short description|Cluster of kofun Kofuns in Ōno, Chūbu, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | name = No Kofun Group | native_name = 野古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = No kofun Group CCB1987-1C2A9.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = No Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = No Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|48|N|136|37|31|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōno, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th to early 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''No Kofun Group'''|野古墳群| No Kofun-gun}} is cluster of small ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Ono neighborhood of the town of [[Ōno, Gifu|Ōno]], [[Ibi District, Gifu|Ibi District]], [[Gifu Prefecture]] in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190832|title= 下船塚古墳 |trans-title= No Kofun Group |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==Overview== The ''kofun'' group is located in the northwestern corner of the [[Nōbi Plain]] in northwestern Gifu Prefecture, surrounded by [[paddy field|rice fields]] and orchards. In a roughly one square kilometer area, there were once over 200 tumuli, of which 14 theoretically received protection in 1957 as a National Historic Site. These included 27 [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumuli]] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, or the similarly-shaped "{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}" ''kofun'' {{nihongo|''hotategai-gata-kofun''|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}). At present, only nine remain; with the others being destroyed for expansion of farm land. Of these remaining nine, eight have been excavated, and from the [[grave goods]] recovered (''[[haniwa]]'', [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, horse fittings, iron and wooden implements, iron swords and spearheads), were determined to date to the middle of the 5th century to the early part of the 6th century AD. One of the bronze mirrors is kept at the [[Gotoh Museum]] in [[Tokyo]]. The site is about eight minutes by car from [[Motosu Station]] on the [[Tarumi Railway Tarumi Line]].{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class=wikitable ! #||name ||photo ||type & Dimensions || Comments |- ||1||Motare Kofun
(モタレ古墳) ||[[file:No Kofungun 1go-fun.jpg|100px]]|| keyhole-shaped
54m (L) x 36m (D) x 6m (H) ||Originally thought to be circular, excavations in 1989 confirmed that it was a moated keyhole-shaped tomb. Fragments of ''haniwa'', [[Sue ware]] and a wooden plough were recovered. |- ||2||Fudozuka Kofun
(不動塚古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 2go-fun.jpg|100px]] || keyhole-shaped
64m (L) x 44m (D) x 7m (H) ||Excavations in 1989 found fragments of ''haniwa''; most of the mound has been lost |- |3||Minamiyashiki-nishi Kofun
(南屋敷西古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 3go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||keyhole-shaped
76m (L) x 44m (D) x 5.4m (H) ||Excavations in 1982 found that it was double-moated; fragments of cylindrical haniwa were found |- |4||Noborikoshi Kofun
(墳登越古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 4go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||keyhole-shaped
83m (L) x 52m (D) x 7.3m (H) ||The largest in the group. It was double-moated; fragments of cylindrical ''haniwa'' were found |- |5||Jōtsuka Kofun
(城塚古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 5go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||keyhole-shaped
75m (L) x 39m (D) x 6.2m (H)||In excavations, a Chinese-made bronze mirror, horse harness and a large sword were found in the burial chamber. |- |6||Inui Yashiki Kofun
(乾屋敷古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 6go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||keyhole-shaped
79m (L) x 49m (D) x 6.3m (H)||Thought to be the oldest in the group. Half-destroyed by residential housing; some haniwa have been recovered. |- |7|| -unnamed-||[[file:No Kofungun 7go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||scallop-shaped
29m (L) x 22.6m (D) x 4.2m (H) ||Thought to be a circular tumuli, its design was confirmed in 1923. |- |8|| -unnamed-||[[file:No Kofungun 8go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||square-shaped
15.8 (L) x 15.8m (D) x 3.0m (H) ||Excavated in 1994 |- |9|| -unnamed-||[[file:No Kofungun 9go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||keyhole-shaped
30.5 (L) x 23m (D) x 6m (H) ||Excavated in 1994, ''haniwa'', Sue pottery were uncovered. |- |- |} ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Short description|Cluster of kofun Kofuns in Ōno, Chūbu, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | name = No Kofun Group | native_name = 野古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = No kofun Group CCB1987-1C2A9.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = No Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = No Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|48|N|136|37|31|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōno, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th to early 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''No Kofun Group'''|野古墳群| No Kofun-gun}} is cluster of small ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Ono neighborhood of the town of [[Ōno, Gifu|Ōno]], [[Ibi District, Gifu|Ibi District]], [[Gifu Prefecture]] in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190832|title= 下船塚古墳 |trans-title= No Kofun Group |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==Overview== The ''kofun'' group is located in the northwestern corner of the [[Nōbi Plain]] in northwestern Gifu Prefecture, surrounded by [[paddy field|rice fields]] and orchards. In a roughly one square kilometer area, there were once over 200 tumuli, of which 14 theoretically received protection in 1957 as a National Historic Site. These included 27 [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumuli]] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, or the similarly-shaped "{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}" ''kofun'' {{nihongo|''hotategai-gata-kofun''|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}). At present, only nine remain; with the others being destroyed for expansion of farm land. Of these remaining nine, eight have been excavated, and from the [[grave goods]] recovered (''[[haniwa]]'', [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, horse fittings, iron and wooden implements, iron swords and spearheads), were determined to date to the middle of the 5th century to the early part of the 6th century AD. One of the bronze mirrors is kept at the [[Gotoh Museum]] in [[Tokyo]]. The site is about eight minutes by car from [[Motosu Station]] on the [[Tarumi Railway Tarumi Line]].{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class=wikitable ! #||name ||photo ||type & Dimensions || Comments |- ||1||Motare Kofun
(モタレ古墳) ||[[file:No Kofungun 1go-fun.jpg|100px]]|| keyhole-shaped
54m (L) x 36m (D) x 6m (H) ||Originally thought to be circular, excavations in 1989 confirmed that it was a moated keyhole-shaped tomb. Fragments of ''haniwa'', [[Sue pottery]] and a wooden plough were recovered. |- ||2||Fudozuka Kofun
(不動塚古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 2go-fun.jpg|100px]] || keyhole-shaped
64m (L) x 44m (D) x 7m (H) ||Excavations in 1989 found fragments of ''haniwa''; most of the mound has been lost |- |3||Minamiyashiki-nishi Kofun
(南屋敷西古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 3go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||keyhole-shaped
76m (L) x 44m (D) x 5.4m (H) ||Excavations in 1982 found that it was double-moated; fragments of cylindrical haniwa were found |- |4||Noborikoshi Kofun
(墳登越古墳)||[[file:No Kofungun 4go-fun.jpg|100px]] ||keyhole-shaped
