{{ill|Ono|WD=Q1203308}}| region = {{ill|Chūbu region|en|Chūbu region|WD=Q134638|lt=Chūbu region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th to early 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''No Kofun Group'''|野古墳群| No Kofun-gun}} is cluster of small ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Ono neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Ōno, Gifu|en|Ōno, Gifu|WD=Q1203308|lt=Ōno}}, {{ill|Ibi District, Gifu|en|Ibi District, Gifu|WD=Q1204309|lt=Ibi District}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}} in the {{ill|Chūbu region|en|Chūbu region|WD=Q134638|lt=Chūbu region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190832|title= 下船塚古墳 |trans-title= No Kofun Group |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==Overview== The ''kofun'' group is located in the northwestern corner of the {{ill|Nōbi Plain|en|Nōbi Plain|WD=Q1204093|lt=Nōbi Plain}} in northwestern Gifu Prefecture, surrounded by {{ill|Paddy field|en|Paddy field|WD=Q842623|lt=rice fields}} and orchards. In a roughly one square kilometer area, there were once over 200 tumuli, of which 14 theoretically received protection in 1957 as a National Historic Site. These included 27 {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped tumuli}} ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}), which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, or the similarly-shaped "{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|lt=scallop-type|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}" ''kofun'' {{nihongo|''hotategai-gata-kofun''|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun}}). At present, only nine remain; with the others being destroyed for expansion of farm land. Of these remaining nine, eight have been excavated, and from the {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} recovered (''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'', [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, horse fittings, iron and wooden implements, iron swords and spearheads), were determined to date to the middle of the 5th century to the early part of the 6th century AD. One of the bronze mirrors is kept at the {{ill|Gotoh Museum|en|Gotoh Museum|WD=Q2374140|lt=Gotoh Museum}} in {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}. The site is about eight minutes by car from {{ill|Motosu Station|en|Motosu Station|WD=Q6918526|lt=Motosu Station}} on the [[:en:Tarumi Railway Tarumi Line|Tarumi Railway Tarumi Line]].{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class=wikitable ! #||name ||photo ||type & Dimensions || Comments |- ||1||Motare Kofun
(モタレ古墳) |||| keyhole-shaped
54m (L) x 36m (D) x 6m (H) ||Originally thought to be circular, excavations in 1989 confirmed that it was a moated keyhole-shaped tomb. Fragments of ''haniwa'', {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} and a wooden plough were recovered. |- ||2||Fudozuka Kofun
(不動塚古墳)|| || keyhole-shaped
64m (L) x 44m (D) x 7m (H) ||Excavations in 1989 found fragments of ''haniwa''; most of the mound has been lost |- |3||Minamiyashiki-nishi Kofun
(南屋敷西古墳)|| ||keyhole-shaped
76m (L) x 44m (D) x 5.4m (H) ||Excavations in 1982 found that it was double-moated; fragments of cylindrical haniwa were found |- |4||Noborikoshi Kofun
(墳登越古墳)|| ||keyhole-shaped
83m (L) x 52m (D) x 7.3m (H) ||The largest in the group. It was double-moated; fragments of cylindrical ''haniwa'' were found |- |5||Jōtsuka Kofun
(城塚古墳)|| ||keyhole-shaped
75m (L) x 39m (D) x 6.2m (H)||In excavations, a Chinese-made bronze mirror, horse harness and a large sword were found in the burial chamber. |- |6||Inui Yashiki Kofun
(乾屋敷古墳)|| ||keyhole-shaped
79m (L) x 49m (D) x 6.3m (H)||Thought to be the oldest in the group. Half-destroyed by residential housing; some haniwa have been recovered. |- |7|| -unnamed-|| ||scallop-shaped
29m (L) x 22.6m (D) x 4.2m (H) ||Thought to be a circular tumuli, its design was confirmed in 1923. |- |8|| -unnamed-|| ||square-shaped
15.8 (L) x 15.8m (D) x 3.0m (H) ||Excavated in 1994 |- |9|| -unnamed-|| ||keyhole-shaped
30.5 (L) x 23m (D) x 6m (H) ||Excavated in 1994, ''haniwa'', Sue pottery were uncovered. |- |- |} ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|WD=Q6573261|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons category-inline}} *[http://www.town-ono.jp/0000000491.html Ono town home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/siseki/nokofunngunn.html Gifu Prefectural home page] {{in lang|ja}} No Kofun Cluster {{Kofun navbar}} [[file:No Kofungun 1go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[file:No Kofungun 2go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[file:No Kofungun 3go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[file:No Kofungun 4go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[file:No Kofungun 5go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[file:No Kofungun 6go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[file:No Kofungun 7go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[file:No Kofungun 8go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[file:No Kofungun 9go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[en:No Kofun Cluster]] {{Commons category|No Kofun Group}} [[ja:野古墳群]] {{wikidata link|Q11645238}} [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:History of Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed parentheses]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

30.5 (L) x 23m (D) x 6m (H) ||Excavated in 1994, ''haniwa'', Sue pottery were uncovered. |- |- |} ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|WD=Q6573261|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons category-inline}} *[http://www.town-ono.jp/0000000491.html Ono town home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/siseki/nokofunngunn.html Gifu Prefectural home page] {{in lang|ja}} No Kofun Cluster {{Kofun navbar}} [[file:No Kofungun 1go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[file:No Kofungun 2go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[file:No Kofungun 3go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[file:No Kofungun 4go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[file:No Kofungun 5go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[file:No Kofungun 6go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[file:No Kofungun 7go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[file:No Kofungun 8go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[file:No Kofungun 9go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[en:No Kofun Cluster]] {{Commons category|No Kofun Group}} [[ja:野古墳群]] {{wikidata link|Q11645238}} [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:History of Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed parentheses]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:No Kofun Group]][[en:No Kofun Cluster]][[ja:野古墳群]] [[co:commons:Category:No Kofun Group]][[en:No Kofun Cluster]][[ja:野古墳群]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:No Kofun Group]][[en:No Kofun Cluster]][[ja:野古墳群]] {{Short description|Cluster of kofun Kofuns in Ōno, Chūbu, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = No Kofun Group | native_name = 野古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =No kofun Group CCB1987-1C2A9.jpg; File:No kofun Group CCB1987-1C2A9.jpg| image_size = | alt = | caption = No Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = No Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|35|28|48|N|136|37|31|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|35.48|N|136.62527778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location ={{ill|Ōno, Gifu|en|Ōno, Gifu|WD=Q1203308|lt=Ōno, Gifu|1=Ōno, Gifu}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}; {{ill|Ono|WD=Q1203308|1=Ōno, Gifu}}| region = {{ill|Chūbu region|en|Chūbu region|WD=Q134638|lt=Chūbu region|1=Chūbu region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th to early 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''No Kofun Group'''|野古墳群| No Kofun-gun}} is cluster of small ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Ono neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Ōno, Gifu|en|Ōno, Gifu|WD=Q1203308|lt=Ōno|1=Ōno, Gifu}}, {{ill|Ibi District, Gifu|en|Ibi District, Gifu|WD=Q1204309|lt=Ibi District|1=Ibi District, Gifu}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}} in the {{ill|Chūbu region|en|Chūbu region|WD=Q134638|lt=Chūbu region|1=Chūbu region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190832|title= 下船塚古墳 |trans-title= No Kofun Group |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==Overview== The ''kofun'' group is located in the northwestern corner of the {{ill|Nōbi Plain|en|Nōbi Plain|WD=Q1204093|lt=Nōbi Plain|1=Nōbi Plain}} in northwestern Gifu Prefecture, surrounded by {{ill|Paddy field|en|Paddy field|WD=Q842623|lt=rice fields|1=Paddy field}} and orchards. In a roughly one square kilometer area, there were once over 200 tumuli, of which 14 theoretically received protection in 1957 as a National Historic Site. These included 27 {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped tumuli|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}), which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, or the similarly-shaped "{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|lt=scallop-type|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}" ''kofun'' {{nihongo|''hotategai-gata-kofun''|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun}}). At present, only nine remain; with the others being destroyed for expansion of farm land. Of these remaining nine, eight have been excavated, and from the {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} recovered (''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'', [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, horse fittings, iron and wooden implements, iron swords and spearheads), were determined to date to the middle of the 5th century to the early part of the 6th century AD. One of the bronze mirrors is kept at the {{ill|Gotoh Museum|en|Gotoh Museum|WD=Q2374140|lt=Gotoh Museum|1=Gotoh Museum}} in {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}. The site is about eight minutes by car from {{ill|Motosu Station|en|Motosu Station|WD=Q6918526|lt=Motosu Station|1=Motosu Station}} on the [[:en:Tarumi Railway Tarumi Line|Tarumi Railway Tarumi Line]].{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class=wikitable ! #||name ||photo ||type & Dimensions || Comments |- ||1||Motare Kofun
(モタレ古墳) |||| keyhole-shaped
54m (L) x 36m (D) x 6m (H) ||Originally thought to be circular, excavations in 1989 confirmed that it was a moated keyhole-shaped tomb. Fragments of ''haniwa'', {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} and a wooden plough were recovered. |- ||2||Fudozuka Kofun
(不動塚古墳)|| || keyhole-shaped
64m (L) x 44m (D) x 7m (H) ||Excavations in 1989 found fragments of ''haniwa''; most of the mound has been lost |- |3||Minamiyashiki-nishi Kofun
(南屋敷西古墳)|| ||keyhole-shaped
76m (L) x 44m (D) x 5.4m (H) ||Excavations in 1982 found that it was double-moated; fragments of cylindrical haniwa were found |- |4||Noborikoshi Kofun
(墳登越古墳)|| ||keyhole-shaped
83m (L) x 52m (D) x 7.3m (H) ||The largest in the group. It was double-moated; fragments of cylindrical ''haniwa'' were found |- |5||Jōtsuka Kofun
(城塚古墳)|| ||keyhole-shaped
75m (L) x 39m (D) x 6.2m (H)||In excavations, a Chinese-made bronze mirror, horse harness and a large sword were found in the burial chamber. |- |6||Inui Yashiki Kofun
(乾屋敷古墳)|| ||keyhole-shaped
79m (L) x 49m (D) x 6.3m (H)||Thought to be the oldest in the group. Half-destroyed by residential housing; some haniwa have been recovered. |- |7|| -unnamed-|| ||scallop-shaped
29m (L) x 22.6m (D) x 4.2m (H) ||Thought to be a circular tumuli, its design was confirmed in 1923. |- |8|| -unnamed-|| ||square-shaped
15.8 (L) x 15.8m (D) x 3.0m (H) ||Excavated in 1994 |- |9|| -unnamed-|| ||keyhole-shaped
30.5 (L) x 23m (D) x 6m (H) ||Excavated in 1994, ''haniwa'', Sue pottery were uncovered. |- |- |} ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|WD=Q6573261|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons category-inline}} *[http://www.town-ono.jp/0000000491.html Ono town home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/siseki/nokofunngunn.html Gifu Prefectural home page] {{in lang|ja}} No Kofun Cluster {{Kofun navbar}} [[file:No Kofungun 1go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[file:No Kofungun 2go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[file:No Kofungun 3go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[file:No Kofungun 4go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[file:No Kofungun 5go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[file:No Kofungun 6go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[file:No Kofungun 7go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[file:No Kofungun 8go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[file:No Kofungun 9go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[en:No Kofun Cluster]] {{Commons category|No Kofun Group}} [[ja:野古墳群]] {{wikidata link|Q11645238}} [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:History of Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed parentheses]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages]]

{{ill|Ono|WD=Q1203308|1=Ōno, Gifu}}| region = {{ill|Chūbu region|en|Chūbu region|WD=Q134638|lt=Chūbu region|1=Chūbu region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th to early 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''No Kofun Group'''|野古墳群| No Kofun-gun}} is cluster of small ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Ono neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Ōno, Gifu|en|Ōno, Gifu|WD=Q1203308|lt=Ōno|1=Ōno, Gifu}}, {{ill|Ibi District, Gifu|en|Ibi District, Gifu|WD=Q1204309|lt=Ibi District|1=Ibi District, Gifu}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}} in the {{ill|Chūbu region|en|Chūbu region|WD=Q134638|lt=Chūbu region|1=Chūbu region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190832|title= 下船塚古墳 |trans-title= No Kofun Group |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==Overview== The ''kofun'' group is located in the northwestern corner of the {{ill|Nōbi Plain|en|Nōbi Plain|WD=Q1204093|lt=Nōbi Plain|1=Nōbi Plain}} in northwestern Gifu Prefecture, surrounded by {{ill|Paddy field|en|Paddy field|WD=Q842623|lt=rice fields|1=Paddy field}} and orchards. In a roughly one square kilometer area, there were once over 200 tumuli, of which 14 theoretically received protection in 1957 as a National Historic Site. These included 27 {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped tumuli|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}), which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, or the similarly-shaped "{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|lt=scallop-type|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}" ''kofun'' {{nihongo|''hotategai-gata-kofun''|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun}}). At present, only nine remain; with the others being destroyed for expansion of farm land. Of these remaining nine, eight have been excavated, and from the {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} recovered (''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'', [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, horse fittings, iron and wooden implements, iron swords and spearheads), were determined to date to the middle of the 5th century to the early part of the 6th century AD. One of the bronze mirrors is kept at the {{ill|Gotoh Museum|en|Gotoh Museum|WD=Q2374140|lt=Gotoh Museum|1=Gotoh Museum}} in {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}. The site is about eight minutes by car from {{ill|Motosu Station|en|Motosu Station|WD=Q6918526|lt=Motosu Station|1=Motosu Station}} on the [[:en:Tarumi Railway Tarumi Line|Tarumi Railway Tarumi Line]].{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class=wikitable ! #||name ||photo ||type & Dimensions || Comments |- ||1||Motare Kofun
(モタレ古墳) |||| keyhole-shaped
54m (L) x 36m (D) x 6m (H) ||Originally thought to be circular, excavations in 1989 confirmed that it was a moated keyhole-shaped tomb. Fragments of ''haniwa'', {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} and a wooden plough were recovered. |- ||2||Fudozuka Kofun
(不動塚古墳)|| || keyhole-shaped
64m (L) x 44m (D) x 7m (H) ||Excavations in 1989 found fragments of ''haniwa''; most of the mound has been lost |- |3||Minamiyashiki-nishi Kofun
(南屋敷西古墳)|| ||keyhole-shaped
76m (L) x 44m (D) x 5.4m (H) ||Excavations in 1982 found that it was double-moated; fragments of cylindrical haniwa were found |- |4||Noborikoshi Kofun
(墳登越古墳)|| ||keyhole-shaped
83m (L) x 52m (D) x 7.3m (H) ||The largest in the group. It was double-moated; fragments of cylindrical ''haniwa'' were found |- |5||Jōtsuka Kofun
(城塚古墳)|| ||keyhole-shaped
75m (L) x 39m (D) x 6.2m (H)||In excavations, a Chinese-made bronze mirror, horse harness and a large sword were found in the burial chamber. |- |6||Inui Yashiki Kofun
(乾屋敷古墳)|| ||keyhole-shaped
79m (L) x 49m (D) x 6.3m (H)||Thought to be the oldest in the group. Half-destroyed by residential housing; some haniwa have been recovered. |- |7|| -unnamed-|| ||scallop-shaped
29m (L) x 22.6m (D) x 4.2m (H) ||Thought to be a circular tumuli, its design was confirmed in 1923. |- |8|| -unnamed-|| ||square-shaped
15.8 (L) x 15.8m (D) x 3.0m (H) ||Excavated in 1994 |- |9|| -unnamed-|| ||keyhole-shaped
30.5 (L) x 23m (D) x 6m (H) ||Excavated in 1994, ''haniwa'', Sue pottery were uncovered. |- |- |} ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|WD=Q6573261|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons category-inline}} *[http://www.town-ono.jp/0000000491.html Ono town home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/siseki/nokofunngunn.html Gifu Prefectural home page] {{in lang|ja}} No Kofun Cluster {{Kofun navbar}} [[file:No Kofungun 1go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[file:No Kofungun 2go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[file:No Kofungun 3go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[file:No Kofungun 4go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[file:No Kofungun 5go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[file:No Kofungun 6go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[file:No Kofungun 7go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[file:No Kofungun 8go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[file:No Kofungun 9go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[en:No Kofun Cluster]] {{Commons category|No Kofun Group}} [[ja:野古墳群]] {{wikidata link|Q11645238}} [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:History of Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed parentheses]]

(墳登越古墳)|| ||keyhole-shaped
83m (L) x 52m (D) x 7.3m (H) ||The largest in the group. It was double-moated; fragments of cylindrical ''haniwa'' were found |- |5||Jōtsuka Kofun
(城塚古墳)|| ||keyhole-shaped

(乾屋敷古墳)|| ||keyhole-shaped
79m (L) x 49m (D) x 6.3m (H)||Thought to be the oldest in the group. Half-destroyed by residential housing; some haniwa have been recovered. |- |7|| -unnamed-|| ||scallop-shaped
29m (L) x 22.6m (D) x 4.2m (H) ||Thought to be a circular tumuli, its design was confirmed in 1923. |- |8|| -unnamed-|| ||square-shaped
15.8 (L) x 15.8m (D) x 3.0m (H) ||Excavated in 1994 |- |9|| -unnamed-|| ||keyhole-shaped
30.5 (L) x 23m (D) x 6m (H) ||Excavated in 1994, ''haniwa'', Sue pottery were uncovered. |- |- |} ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|WD=Q6573261|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons category-inline}} *[http://www.town-ono.jp/0000000491.html Ono town home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/siseki/nokofunngunn.html Gifu Prefectural home page] {{in lang|ja}} No Kofun Cluster {{Kofun navbar}} [[file:No Kofungun 1go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[file:No Kofungun 2go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[file:No Kofungun 3go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[file:No Kofungun 4go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[file:No Kofungun 5go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[file:No Kofungun 6go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[file:No Kofungun 7go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[file:No Kofungun 8go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[file:No Kofungun 9go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[en:No Kofun Cluster]] {{Commons category|No Kofun Group}} [[ja:野古墳群]] {{wikidata link|Q11645238}} [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:History of Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:No Kofun Group]][[en:No Kofun Cluster]][[ja:野古墳群]] [[co:commons:Category:No Kofun Group]][[en:No Kofun Cluster]][[ja:野古墳群]] [[en:No Kofun Cluster]][[ja:野古墳群]] {{Short description|Cluster of kofun Kofuns in Ōno, Chūbu, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = No Kofun Group | native_name = 野古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =No kofun Group CCB1987-1C2A9.jpg; File:No kofun Group CCB1987-1C2A9.jpg| image_size = | alt = | caption = No Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = No Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|35|28|48|N|136|37|31|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|35.48|N|136.62527778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location ={{ill|Ōno, Gifu|en|Ōno, Gifu|WD=Q1203308|lt=Ōno, Gifu|1=Ōno, Gifu}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}; {{ill|Ono|WD=Q1203308|1=Ōno, Gifu}}| region = {{ill|Chūbu region|en|Chūbu region|WD=Q134638|lt=Chūbu region|1=Chūbu region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th to early 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''No Kofun Group'''|野古墳群| No Kofun-gun}} is cluster of small ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Ono neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Ōno, Gifu|en|Ōno, Gifu|WD=Q1203308|lt=Ōno|1=Ōno, Gifu}}, {{ill|Ibi District, Gifu|en|Ibi District, Gifu|WD=Q1204309|lt=Ibi District|1=Ibi District, Gifu}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}} in the {{ill|Chūbu region|en|Chūbu region|WD=Q134638|lt=Chūbu region|1=Chūbu region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190832|title= 下船塚古墳 |trans-title= No Kofun Group |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==Overview== The ''kofun'' group is located in the northwestern corner of the {{ill|Nōbi Plain|en|Nōbi Plain|WD=Q1204093|lt=Nōbi Plain|1=Nōbi Plain}} in northwestern Gifu Prefecture, surrounded by {{ill|Paddy field|en|Paddy field|WD=Q842623|lt=rice fields|1=Paddy field}} and orchards. In a roughly one square kilometer area, there were once over 200 tumuli, of which 14 theoretically received protection in 1957 as a National Historic Site. These included 27 {{ill|Zenpōkōenfun|en|Zenpōkōenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped tumuli|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpōkōenfun|1=[[Zenpōkōenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}), which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, or the similarly-shaped "{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|lt=scallop-type|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}" ''kofun'' {{nihongo|''hotategai-gata-kofun''|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun}}). At present, only nine remain; with the others being destroyed for expansion of farm land. Of these remaining nine, eight have been excavated, and from the {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} recovered (''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'', [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, horse fittings, iron and wooden implements, iron swords and spearheads), were determined to date to the middle of the 5th century to the early part of the 6th century AD. One of the bronze mirrors is kept at the {{ill|Gotoh Museum|en|Gotoh Museum|WD=Q2374140|lt=Gotoh Museum|1=Gotoh Museum}} in {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}. The site is about eight minutes by car from {{ill|Motosu Station|en|Motosu Station|WD=Q6918526|lt=Motosu Station|1=Motosu Station}} on the [[:en:Tarumi Railway Tarumi Line|Tarumi Railway Tarumi Line]].{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class=wikitable ! #||name ||photo ||type & Dimensions || Comments |- ||1||Motare Kofun
(モタレ古墳) |||| keyhole-shaped
54m (L) x 36m (D) x 6m (H) ||Originally thought to be circular, excavations in 1989 confirmed that it was a moated keyhole-shaped tomb. Fragments of ''haniwa'', {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} and a wooden plough were recovered. |- ||2||Fudozuka Kofun
(不動塚古墳)|| || keyhole-shaped
64m (L) x 44m (D) x 7m (H) ||Excavations in 1989 found fragments of ''haniwa''; most of the mound has been lost |- |3||Minamiyashiki-nishi Kofun
(南屋敷西古墳)|| ||keyhole-shaped
76m (L) x 44m (D) x 5.4m (H) ||Excavations in 1982 found that it was double-moated; fragments of cylindrical haniwa were found |- |4||Noborikoshi Kofun
(墳登越古墳)|| ||keyhole-shaped
83m (L) x 52m (D) x 7.3m (H) ||The largest in the group. It was double-moated; fragments of cylindrical ''haniwa'' were found |- |5||Jōtsuka Kofun
(城塚古墳)|| ||keyhole-shaped
75m (L) x 39m (D) x 6.2m (H)||In excavations, a Chinese-made bronze mirror, horse harness and a large sword were found in the burial chamber. |- |6||Inui Yashiki Kofun
(乾屋敷古墳)|| ||keyhole-shaped
79m (L) x 49m (D) x 6.3m (H)||Thought to be the oldest in the group. Half-destroyed by residential housing; some haniwa have been recovered. |- |7|| -unnamed-|| ||scallop-shaped
29m (L) x 22.6m (D) x 4.2m (H) ||Thought to be a circular tumuli, its design was confirmed in 1923. |- |8|| -unnamed-|| ||square-shaped
15.8 (L) x 15.8m (D) x 3.0m (H) ||Excavated in 1994 |- |9|| -unnamed-|| ||keyhole-shaped
30.5 (L) x 23m (D) x 6m (H) ||Excavated in 1994, ''haniwa'', Sue pottery were uncovered. |- |- |} ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|WD=Q6573261|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons category-inline}} *[http://www.town-ono.jp/0000000491.html Ono town home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/siseki/nokofunngunn.html Gifu Prefectural home page] {{in lang|ja}} No Kofun Cluster {{Kofun navbar}} [[file:No Kofungun 1go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[file:No Kofungun 2go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[file:No Kofungun 3go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[file:No Kofungun 4go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[file:No Kofungun 5go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[file:No Kofungun 6go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[file:No Kofungun 7go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[file:No Kofungun 8go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[file:No Kofungun 9go-fun.jpg|100px]] [[en:No Kofun Cluster]] {{Commons category|No Kofun Group}} [[ja:野古墳群]] {{wikidata link|Q11645238}} [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:History of Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

{{ill|Ono|WD=Q1203308|1=Ōno, Gifu}}| region = {{ill|Chūbu region|en|Chūbu region|WD=Q134638|lt=Chūbu region|1=Chūbu region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th to early 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''No Kofun Group'''|野古墳群| No Kofun-gun}} is cluster of small ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Ono neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Ōno, Gifu|en|Ōno, Gifu|WD=Q1203308|lt=Ōno|1=Ōno, Gifu}}, {{ill|Ibi District, Gifu|en|Ibi District, Gifu|WD=Q1204309|lt=Ibi District|1=Ibi District, Gifu}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}} in the {{ill|Chūbu region|en|Chūbu region|WD=Q134638|lt=Chūbu region|1=Chūbu region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190832|title= 下船塚古墳 |trans-title= No Kofun Group |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==Overview== The ''kofun'' group is located in the northwestern corner of the {{ill|Nōbi Plain|en|Nōbi Plain|WD=Q1204093|lt=Nōbi Plain|1=Nōbi Plain}} in northwestern Gifu Prefecture, surrounded by {{ill|Paddy field|en|Paddy field|WD=Q842623|lt=rice fields|1=Paddy field}} and orchards. In a roughly one square kilometer area, there were once over 200 tumuli, of which 14 theoretically received protection in 1957 as a National Historic Site. These included 27 {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped tumuli|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}} ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=[[Zenpō-kōen-fun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}), which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, or the similarly-shaped "{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|lt=scallop-type|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}" ''kofun'' {{nihongo|''hotategai-gata-kofun''|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun}}). At present, only nine remain; with the others being destroyed for expansion of farm land. Of these remaining nine, eight have been excavated, and from the {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} recovered (''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'', [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, horse fittings, iron and wooden implements, iron swords and spearheads), were determined to date to the middle of the 5th century to the early part of the 6th century AD. One of the bronze mirrors is kept at the {{ill|Gotoh Museum|en|Gotoh Museum|WD=Q2374140|lt=Gotoh Museum|1=Gotoh Museum}} in {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}. The site is about eight minutes by car from {{ill|Motosu Station|en|Motosu Station|WD=Q6918526|lt=Motosu Station|1=Motosu Station}} on the [[:en:Tarumi Railway Tarumi Line|Tarumi Railway Tarumi Line]].{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class=wikitable ! #||name ||photo ||type & Dimensions || Comments |- ||1||Motare Kofun
